Hey, welcome back and welcome to the module on the power of vision focus, which is basically the X factor that ensures massive success in all aspects of your life and especially in sales where it's absolutely crucial. In fact, I'm going to go out on the limb here right out of the gate and say that unless you become at least reasonably proficient at harnessing the power of vision focus, then it's going to be nearly impossible for you to achieve the level of sales success that you truly can't believe. Now that being said, if you're like most people, you've probably heard the words vision and focus used independently of each other when it comes to both sales and in general business terms. You're probably also familiar with the concept of how crucial is to develop a compelling vision for your future to know exactly where it is you want to go in life and how to be able to visualize yourself in the future being complete and successful and to feel empowered by that and inspired at a very deep and intimate level. It's also the point where you can touch it and taste it and feel it in your own mind. That's what a really compelling vision does for you. And likewise, you've also probably heard of how important it is to be able to keep yourself focused on what you do want to go in life versus what you're afraid of, which is what most unsuccessful people do. You see as a result of their fears, they actually focus when they don't want to go as opposed to where they do want to go and then the heading in the exact wrong direction. You see, it's one of the most fundamental laws of nature that we always end up going in the direction of our focus. So if you're constantly focusing where you don't want to go, that's precisely where you're going to end up. So you have vision on one side of the equation and focus on the other. And when you combine those two together into one single competency, you end up with something very, very powerful, almost magical in fact. Namely, you end up virtually ensuring your own success by giving yourself a precise target in life to aim for and then the daily discipline to focus on that target despite the challenges that pop up, thereby ensuring that you definitely end up getting there. You have your ability to have a vision for your future and focus on that vision. It's called vision focus. Essentially to know where you want to go in life, to have a vision for your future, not a goal of vision. And to set that vision and make it so clear in your mind that you can taste it, touch it, feel it, it inspires you every day and helps you get out of bed. It makes you jump out of bed, go to work and do what you've got to do for the bigger picture of where you're going in life, your vision for the future. People remember, if they're trying to motivate people, they won't follow your goals. They follow vision. Every human being is thirsty for a vision and very few human beings have one. So what do they do? They gravitate towards someone who has a vision and jump on someone else's bandwagon. The chiefs are the Indians. The chiefs have a vision. To be successful in life, you have to have a vision for your future. It's beyond the goal. It's a vision. And with that is understanding what's called your why. You know, why are you going out and doing what you're doing? Is it just for money? It can't be. Your why is it for your family, your community, for your spiritual life, things you believe in, whatever. You'll always, you know, as human beings, the way we're built, we'll always do more for someone we love than for ourselves. We'll go the extra mile for our child or our spouse. We truly love them. But for the people that depend us, we'll even work harder for them than for us to understand that power, to know that your vision is not just about you. It's about the people around you as well. And when you get hooked into that why, it's incredibly empowering. People say, oh, you know, I have trouble staying motivated. I find it so hard to get motivated. Yeah? Well, guess what? You don't have a very powerful why then. You don't know why you're doing what you're doing. If you truly had a powerful why in your life, you really knew what you were doing, you'd be inspired. You actually knew what it was you were seeking at the highest level, not money. Money is just the end result that solves problems. If it's money, it's to take it and buy a house so you can protect your family, live in a nice neighborhood, put your daughter or your son through school, retire with pride and dignity with your wife and your love. That's what money buys, not money itself. It's the why is much deeper than money.
